RECORD.HEADING: Chapter 6: Reporting on Demographic Data in New York City Public Schools
RECORD.PARA: Chapter 6: Reporting on Demographic Data in New York City Public Schools

RECORD.HEADING: § 21-956 Definitions.
RECORD.PARA: § 21-956 Definitions.
For the purposes of this chapter, the following terms have the following meanings: [ALP S-044]
Over the counter. The term "over the counter" means a process of enrollment for high school students other than the citywide high school admissions process. [ALP S-044]

Performance level. The term "performance level" means the classification of test scores received on the New York state English language arts and mathematics examinations into four proficiency categories as reported by the state. [ALP S-044]
Resident in temporary housing. The term "resident in temporary housing" means satisfying the definition of "homeless child" as set forth in chancellor's regulation A-780. [ALP S-044]

School. The term "school" means a school of the city school district of the city of New York, including district 75 programs, district 79 programs, and charter schools under the jurisdiction of the department. [ALP S-091]

Special programs. The term "special programs" means academic programs including but not limited to gifted and talented programs in grades kindergarten through five and dual language programs in grades kindergarten through eight. [ALP S-044]

Student in foster care. The term “student in foster care” means a student placed in 24-hour substitute care away from their birth or adoptive parents and for whom the child welfare agency has placement and care responsibility, as set forth in chancellor’s regulation A-101, at any point and for any length of time during the school year. [ALP S-091]

RECORD.HEADING: § 21-957 Annual report on the demographics of students in grades kindergarten through 8.
RECORD.PARA: § 21-957 Annual report on the demographics of students in grades kindergarten through 8.

Not later than December 31, 2015, and by December 1 of each year thereafter, the department shall submit to the speaker of the council and post on its website a report regarding the following: [ALP S-044]

a.For each community school district, school within such district, special program within such school, and grade within such school, the total number of students enrolled in the preceding school year in grades kindergarten through 8 and the number and percentage of such students who: [ALP S-096]
1.Receive special education services; [ALP S-044]
2.Are English language learners; [ALP S-044]
3.Are eligible for the federal free or reduced price meals program; [ALP S-044]
4.Reside in temporary housing; [ALP S-044]
5.Are in foster care; [ALP S-091]
6.Are attending school out of the attendance zone in which the student resides; and [ALP S-091]
7.Are attending school out of the community school district in which the student resides. [ALP S-091]
b.The data provided pursuant to subdivision a shall be disaggregated by: [ALP S-044]
1.Grade level; [ALP S-044]
2.Race or ethnicity; [ALP S-044]
3.Gender; [ALP S-044]
4.Special education status; [ALP S-091]
5.English language learner status; and [ALP S-091]
6.Primary home language. [ALP S-091]

c.For each community school district, each borough, and citywide, the number and percentage of students enrolled in special programs in the preceding school year in grades kindergarten through 8 and the number and percentage of students who: [ALP S-096]
1.Receive special education services; [ALP S-096]
2.Are English language learners; [ALP S-096]
3.Are eligible for the federal free or reduced price meals program; [ALP S-096]
4.Reside in temporary housing; [ALP S-096]
5.Are in foster care; [ALP S-096]
6.Are attending school out of the attendance zone in which the student resides; and [ALP S-096]
7.Are attending school out of the community school district in which the student resides. [ALP S-096]
d.The data provided pursuant to subdivision c shall be disaggregated by: [ALP S-096]
1.Program type; [ALP S-096]
2.Grade level; [ALP S-096]
3.Race or ethnicity; [ALP S-096]
4.Gender; [ALP S-096]
5.Special education status; and [ALP S-096]
6.English language learner status. [ALP S-096]
e.For students in grades 3 through 8, the data provided pursuant to subdivision a of this section shall indicate: [ALP S-096]
1.The number of students who completed the New York state mathematics examination, disaggregated by performance level; and [ALP S-044]
2.The number of students who completed the New York state English language arts examination, disaggregated by performance level. [ALP S-044]
f.For each school and special program set forth in subdivision a of this section, the department shall report: [ALP S-096]

1.The admissions process used by such school or special program, including but not limited to, whether admission to such school or special program is based on a lottery; a geographic zone; an audition; a screening of candidates for such school; including a detailed description of such screening; or a standardized test; [ALP S-044]

2.Any criteria or methods that are used to supplement the admissions process, including but not limited to, preferences established under the department's diversity in admissions pilot, composite score formulas, waitlists or a principal's discretion; [ALP S-044]

3.A side-by-side comparison of the racial and ethnic demographics of such school or special program with the racial and ethnic demographics of all students in grades kindergarten through 8 that reside within the applicable attendance zone, and, if the applicable attendance zone is smaller than the community school district, a side-by-side comparison of the racial and ethnic demographics of the school or special program, the applicable attendance zone and the applicable community school district; and [ALP S-096]

4.Whether such school or special program is becoming more or less similar to the racial and ethnic demographics of the applicable attendance zone and the community school district, based on the comparison required pursuant to paragraph 3 of this subdivision. [ALP S-044]

g.For each community school district, the department shall report on whether the department made any efforts in such community school district during the preceding school year to encourage a diverse student body in its schools and special programs and, if so, the details of such efforts, including, but not limited to, strategic site selection of new schools and special programs, making recommendations to the community education council to draw attendance zones with recognition of the demographics of neighborhoods, the allocation of resources for schools and special programs, and targeted outreach and recruitment efforts. [ALP S-096]

h.No information that is otherwise required to be reported pursuant to this section shall be reported in a manner that would violate any applicable provision of federal, state or local law relating to the privacy of student information or that would interfere with law enforcement investigations or otherwise conflict with the interests of law enforcement. If a category contains between 1 and 5 students, or contains an amount that would allow another category that contains between 1 and 5 students to be deduced, the number shall be replaced with a symbol. A category that contains 0 shall be reported as 0, unless such reporting would violate any applicable provision of federal, state or local law relating to the privacy of student information. [ALP S-096]
i.The report required pursuant to this section shall, to the extent the department has such information, include data regarding charter schools located within the 5 boroughs. [ALP S-096]

RECORD.HEADING: § 21-957.1 Annual report on high school student demographics.
RECORD.PARA: § 21-957.1 Annual report on high school student demographics.

Not later than December 31, 2015, and by December 1 of each year thereafter, the department shall submit to the speaker of the council and post on its website a report regarding the following: [ALP S-044]
a.For each public high school, the total number of students enrolled in grades nine through twelve in the preceding school year and the number and percentage of such students who: [ALP S-044]
1.Receive special education services; [ALP S-044]
2.Are English language learners; [ALP S-044]
3.Are eligible for the federal free or reduced price meals program; [ALP S-044]
4.Reside in temporary housing; [ALP S-091]
5.Are in foster care; and [ALP S-091]
6.Are enrolled over the counter. [ALP S-091]
b.The data provided pursuant to subdivision a shall be disaggregated by: [ALP S-044]
1.Grade level; [ALP S-044]
2.Race or ethnicity; [ALP S-044]
3.Gender; [ALP S-044]
4.Special education status; [ALP S-091]
5.English language learner status; and [ALP S-091]
6.Primary home language. [ALP S-091]
c.For students in grade nine, the data provided pursuant to subdivision a of this section shall provide: [ALP S-044]
1.The number of students who completed the New York state mathematics examination administered in eighth grade, disaggregated by performance level; and [ALP S-044]
2.The number of students who completed the New York state English language arts examination administered in eighth grade, disaggregated by performance level. [ALP S-044]
d.For each high school set forth in subdivision a of this section, the department shall report: [ALP S-044]

1.The admissions process used by such school, such as whether admissions to such school is based on a lottery; a geographic zone; an audition; a screening of candidates for such school, including a detailed description of such screening; or a standardized test; and [ALP S-044]

2.Whether other criteria or methods are used for admissions including, but not limited to, preferences established under the department's diversity in admissions pilot, over the counter admissions, waitlists, or a principal's discretion. [ALP S-044]

e.The department shall report, on an individual high school level, on efforts each individual high school has taken during the preceding school year to encourage a diverse student body including, but not limited to, strategic site selection of new schools and special programs, the allocation of resources for schools and special programs, and targeted outreach and recruitment efforts. [ALP S-044]

f.No information that is otherwise required to be reported pursuant to this section shall be reported in a manner that would violate any applicable provision of federal, state or local law relating to the privacy of student information or that would interfere with law enforcement investigations or otherwise conflict with the interests of law enforcement. If a category contains between one and five students, or contains an amount that would allow another category that contains between one and five students to be deduced, the number shall be replaced with a symbol. A category that contains zero shall be reported as zero, unless such reporting would violate any applicable provision of federal, state or local law relating to the privacy of student information. [ALP S-044]

RECORD.HEADING: § 21-957.2 Annual report on the demographics of students in pre-kindergarten programs operated by the department.
RECORD.PARA: § 21-957.2 Annual report on the demographics of students in pre-kindergarten programs operated by the department.

Not later than November 1, 2016, and by December 1 of each year thereafter, the department shall submit to the speaker of the council and post on its website a report regarding the following: [ALP S-044]

a.For each school that offers a pre-kindergarten program, the total number of students enrolled in the preceding school year in such program, disaggregated by race or ethnicity and gender. [ALP S-044]

b.No information that is otherwise required to be reported pursuant to this section shall be reported in a manner that would violate any applicable provision of federal, state or local law relating to the privacy of student information or that would interfere with law enforcement investigations or otherwise conflict with the interests of law enforcement. If a category contains between one and five students, or contains an amount that would allow another category that contains between one and five students to be deduced, the number shall be replaced with a symbol. A category that contains zero shall be reported as zero, unless such reporting would violate any applicable provision of federal, state or local law relating to the privacy of student information. [ALP S-044]

RECORD.HEADING: § 21-958 Annual report on the demographics of school staff.
RECORD.PARA: § 21-958 Annual report on the demographics of school staff.
a. Definitions. For purposes of this section, the following terms have the following meanings: [ALP S-044]
Leadership staff. The term "leadership staff" means principals and assistant principals employed by the department. [ALP S-044]

Other professional and paraprofessional staff. The term "other professional and paraprofessional staff" means paraprofessionals, therapists and other pedagogical staff employed by the department, including, but not limited to guidance counselors, social workers and school psychologists, that are not teaching staff or leadership staff. [ALP S-044]
School. The term "school" means a school of the city school district of the city of New York. [ALP S-044]
Teaching staff. The term "teaching staff" means teachers employed by the department. [ALP S-044]

b.No later than December 1, 2020, and annually by December 1 thereafter, the department shall submit to the speaker of the council and post on its website a report for the previous school year including the following for each school: [ALP S-044]
1.The district borough number; and [ALP S-044]
2.The number of employees, disaggregated by: [ALP S-044]
(a)Teaching staff; [ALP S-044]
(b)Leadership staff; and [ALP S-044]
(c)Other professional and paraprofessional staff. [ALP S-044]
3.The data reported pursuant to paragraph 2 of this subdivision shall be further disaggregated by: [ALP S-044]
(a)Gender; and [ALP S-044]
(b)Race or ethnicity. [ALP S-044]
4.The data reported pursuant to subparagraphs (a) and (b) of paragraph 3 of this subdivision shall be further disaggregated by: [ALP S-044]
(a)Length of employment at such school; and [ALP S-044]
(b)Years of experience in such position. [ALP S-044]

5.The data reported pursuant to paragraph 2 of this subdivision shall be aggregated by community school district for schools serving students in grades pre-kindergarten through eight, by borough and citywide. [ALP S-044]

c.No information that is otherwise required to be reported pursuant to this section shall be reported in a manner that would violate any applicable provision of federal, state or local law related to the privacy of information or that would interfere with law enforcement investigations or otherwise conflict with the interests of law enforcement. [ALP S-044]
